categories.name: Turn Order
categories.description: Mechanisms that determine the sequence in which players take their actions.

mechanisms.name: Fixed Turn Order
mechanisms.description: Players always take turns in the same sequence (e.g., clockwise).

mechanisms.name: Stat-Based Turn Order
mechanisms.description: Turn order is determined by an in-game attribute or resource count.

mechanisms.name: Auction for Turn Order
mechanisms.description: Players bid resources to determine who goes first.

mechanisms.name: Pass Order
mechanisms.description: The order in which players pass determines the turn order for the next round.

categories.name: Action Selection
categories.description: How players choose what to do on their turn.

mechanisms.name: Action Points
mechanisms.description: Spend a budget of points per turn on various actions with different costs.

mechanisms.name: Worker Placement
mechanisms.description: Assign limited tokens to specific board locations to claim actions and block others.

mechanisms.name: Drafting
mechanisms.description: Select items (usually cards or dice) from a common pool or passing hand.

mechanisms.name: Follow Actions
mechanisms.description: When the active player selects an action, others can perform a weaker version of it.

mechanisms.name: Rondel
mechanisms.description: Actions are arranged in a circle; players move a marker around the circle to select.

mechanisms.name: Bag Building
mechanisms.description: Pull random chips or tokens from a bag to determine available actions or resources.

mechanisms.name: Action Blocking
mechanisms.description: Actions that, once taken, prevent others from taking them in the same round.

mechanisms.name: Simultaneous Action Selection
mechanisms.description: Players secretly choose actions and reveal them at the same time.

mechanisms.name: Auction / Bidding
mechanisms.description: Players compete for resources or turn order by offering increasing amounts of currency.

categories.name: Cards and Deck Management
categories.description: Mechanisms centered around card usage and construction.

mechanisms.name: Deck Building
mechanisms.description: Start with a basic deck and add better cards during play.

mechanisms.name: Hand Management
mechanisms.description: The order, timing, and combination of cards played are critical.

mechanisms.name: Multi-Use Cards
mechanisms.description: Cards can be used for different purposes (e.g., money vs. action vs. resource).

mechanisms.name: Tuck-under Effects
mechanisms.description: Placing cards under another card to upgrade it or store value.

mechanisms.name: Trick-Taking
mechanisms.description: Card play in discrete 'tricks' where the highest card or specific suit wins.

mechanisms.name: Set Collection
mechanisms.description: Collecting groups of identical or related items for points or abilities.

categories.name: Economy and Resources
categories.description: Managing assets, currencies, and engine efficiency.

mechanisms.name: Market
mechanisms.description: Players buy/sell items from a dynamic pool with shifting prices.

mechanisms.name: Resource Conversion
mechanisms.description: Convert Resource A into Resource B (often at a better rate through upgrades).

mechanisms.name: Engine Building
mechanisms.description: Constructing systems where actions become more powerful and efficient over time.

mechanisms.name: Tableau Building
mechanisms.description: Creating a persistent personal area of cards/tiles that grant abilities.

mechanisms.name: Resource Management
mechanisms.description: The strategic accumulation and spending of in-game resources.

mechanisms.name: Income
mechanisms.description: Periodic generation of resources or currency based on game state.

mechanisms.name: Economic Simulation
mechanisms.description: Complex interactions involving production, supply, and demand.

mechanisms.name: Contracts
mechanisms.description: Fulfilling specific resource combinations to gain rewards or points.

mechanisms.name: Area Control / Majority
mechanisms.description: Gain bonuses for having the most influence/units in a specific region.

mechanisms.name: Area Movement
mechanisms.description: Moving pieces between discrete regions on a board.

mechanisms.name: Combat
mechanisms.description: Conflict resolution between entities, often involving dice or cards.

mechanisms.name: Hidden Movement
mechanisms.description: One player tracks their location secretly while others try to hunt them down.

mechanisms.name: Dice Rolling
mechanisms.description: Using polyhedral dice to determine outcomes or generate values.

mechanisms.name: Push Your Luck
mechanisms.description: Deciding whether to take a safe gain or risk it all for more.

mechanisms.name: Roll and Write
mechanisms.description: Rolling dice and recording outcomes on a personal score sheet.

mechanisms.name: Pick-up and Deliver
mechanisms.description: Moving items from one location to another to fulfill objectives.

mechanisms.name: Pattern Building
mechanisms.description: Arranging components in specific spatial configurations for bonuses.

mechanisms.name: Tile Placement
mechanisms.description: Placing physical tiles to build the board or personal area.

categories.name: Information
categories.description: Managing what players know and when.

mechanisms.name: Hidden Information
mechanisms.description: Information known only to some players or none until revealed.

mechanisms.name: Deduction
mechanisms.description: Using logic and available clues to determine secret game states.

mechanisms.name: Variable Player Powers
mechanisms.description: Each player has a unique ability or starting setup.

mechanisms.name: Legacy
mechanisms.description: Permanent changes to the game components or rules across sessions.

mechanisms.name: Campaign
mechanisms.description: A series of linked sessions where state is carried over.

mechanisms.name: Story Driven
mechanisms.description: Narrative elements heavily influence game choices and outcomes.

mechanisms.name: Cooperative Play
mechanisms.description: Players work together to defeat the game engine.

mechanisms.name: Team Play
mechanisms.description: Players are divided into competing groups or teams.

mechanisms.name: Communication Limits
mechanisms.description: Restricting what players can say or signal to one another.

compatibility_notes.note: Efficiency challenge: Action points create scarcity for collection efficiency.
Fog of War: Creates spatial discovery and exploration tension.
High interaction: Prevents opponents from taking critical paths.
Core Loop: The market provides the cards that define the deck's power.
